rulings.Case Title: FARELLA BRAUN + MARTEL LLP VS. ELISABETH L THIERIOT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Vacate Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 25, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT ELISABETH THIERIOT's Motion To Vacate Judgment Respondent Elisabeth Theriot's motion to vacate the judgment filed April 26, 2016 is denied. The motion is untimely per CCP 663a. Assuming without deciding that petitioner Farella Braun + Martel, LLP did not serve a required notice of arbitration as contended by Ms. Theriot, that is not a basis to avoid the strict time limits to seek to vacate a judgment per CCP 663a. The court's records reflect that within the time frame permitted to vacate the judgment Ms. Theriot represented by counsel filed both an application to stay execution of the judgment and a motion to set aside the judgment, showing that she had the ability to and could raise all arguments that she believed appropriate within the time frame of section 663a. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for FBM is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: EXPRESS MESSENGER SYSTEMS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or Tax Memorandum Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 25, 2018, Line 3, PETITIONER EXPRESS MESSENGER SYSTEMS, INC., SUBCONTRACTING CONCEPTS (CT) LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or Tax Memorandum Of Costs Petitioners' motion to strike or tax the memorandum of costs is denied. While there is some conflict in the relevant authorities, the more recent case law establishes that a party who prevails in a lawsuit where the only relief sought is a petition to compel arbitration is a prevailing party for purposes of the costs statutes, at least with respect to costs other than attorney's fees. (See, e.g., Otay River Constructors v. San Diego Expressway (2008)158 Cal. App. 4th 796). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for respondent is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD A DENTON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 25, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendants City and County of San Francisco, Barbara Hale, John Doyle, and Lori Mitchell's motion to strike portions of the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Richard Denton is: a) granted with 20 days leave to amend as to paragraph 62; b) granted without leave to amend as to Ms. Mitchell in the seventh cause of action for harassment and the allegations in the eighth cause of action for failure to prevent discrimination against the individual defendants; and 3) denied as to all other portions of the second amended complaint sought to be stricken. In his supplemental opposition memorandum Mr. Denton has withdrawn his harassment claim against Ms. Mitchell. Mr. Denton has adequately exhausted his administrative remedy for his harassment claim against Ms. Hale by making references to Ms. Hale in the materials he submitted in connection with his DFEH/EEOC charge. Paragraphs 57-61 and 63-66 raise allegations similar to the allegations in the DFEH/EEOC charge. Per Okoli v. Lockheed Technical Operations Co. (1995) 36 Cal.App.4th 1607, Mr. Denton does not need to return to the DFEH every time there is a new incident of discrimination that is similar to what has previously occurred. Paragraph 62 refers to an accommodation request by Mr. Denton following his surgery. Mr. Denton has leave to clarify that the request was related to the medical condition referred to in the DFEH/EEOC charge. The harassment allegations withstand a motion to strike for the same reason that the harassment cause of action withstands defendants' demurrer. The failure to prevent discrimination allegations alleged against Barbara Hale, John Doyle, and Lori Mitchell are stricken because a failure to prevent discrimination claim can only be asserted against an employer. Defendants have withdrawn the portion of their motion seeking to strike defamation allegations. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS DBA KAISER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 25, 2018, Line 11, DEFENDANT THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INC.'s Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Defendant The Permanente Medical Group, Inc.'s motion for judgment on the pleadings as to all causes of action alleged in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Jane Doe is continued to January 8, 2019 to determine if the Bankruptcy Trustee has determined to pursue or abandon the claims alleged in the second amended complaint. Except for further proceedings on this motion and any filing by the Bankruptcy Trustee regarding the Trustee's decision to pursue or abandon the claims alleged in the second amended complaint, all proceedings in this case are stayed until January 8, 2019. The trial date of December 10, 2018 is vacated. A new trial date may be set at the hearing on January 8, 2019. A continuance of this motion, stay of proceedings and vacation of the trial date are necessitated by plaintiff's acknowledgement that at the present time she lacks standing to pursue the claims alleged in the second amended complaint. Both sides may file a brief memorandum no later than January 3, 2019 providing an update on any relevant matters that have occurred since September 25, 2018. rulings.Case Title: FREZGI GEBREZGIABHER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 25, 2018, Line 13, DEFENDANTs LUXOR CABS INCORPORATED, LUXOR EXECUTIVE CAR SERVICE and DANNY NGUYEN's Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Deposition Defendants Luxor Cabs Incorporated, Luxor Executive Car Service, and Danny Nguyen's motion to compel the deposition of plaintiff Frezgi Gebrezgiabher and for monetary sanctions is granted. Mr. Gebrezgiabher must appear for deposition at the office of defendants' counsel on a mutually agreeable date no later than October 1, 2018 and Mr. Gebrezgiabher and his counsel must pay monetary sanctions in the amount of $5,175 to defendants no later than October 25, 2018. The court adopts in full the recommendations and reasons for those recommendations in the report of Judge Pro Tem Conor Moore. Mr. Gebrezgiabher's failure to attend his previously noticed depositions and his contention that the parties did not agree to continue the discovery cutoff date per CCP 2024.060 lack substantial justification. Neither Carpenter v. Superior Court (2006) 141 Cal. App. 4th 249 nor Anderson v. State Personnel Board (1980) 103 Cal. App. 3d 242 have any bearing on section 2024.060 issue. The language used by the parties in their agreement to continue the discovery cutoff date conforms to the language widely used by attorneys throughout the Bay Area and is sufficiently definite to meet the requirements of section 2024.060. There is no reasonable argument that at any time Mr. Mr. Gebrezgiabher was unaware of the date the parties agreed to as the new discovery cutoff date. Because of the patent lack of substantial justification for Mr. Gebrezgiabher's contentions, there is no basis to stay the effect of this ruling pending review of a writ filed by him. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and attaches the report of Judge Pro Tem Conor Moore and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFFS' ASSOCIATION, ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Application Under Rule Of Court 2.550 And Mpa Iso For Order Sealing Records; Declaration Of Harry S. Stern
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 25, 2018, Line 16, PLAINTIFFs SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFFS' ASSOCIATION and JOHN DOE's Application Under Rule Of Court 2.550 for Order Sealing Records Petitioner John Doe's application for an order sealing all pleadings and exhibits is granted in part and denied in part. Petitioner is entitled to an order sealing all exhibits to declarations constituting information protected per Penal Code 832.7. Petitioner has failed to satisfy his burden of showing how his overriding interest in his right to privacy as a peace office would be prejudiced if information other than what is protected by section 832.7 is made available to the public. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for petitioner is required to prepare a proposed order which precisely identifies the information that is protected by section 832.7 and thus to be sealed and complies with CRC 2.550(d) and (e) and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFFS' ASSOCIATION, ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing (On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 25, 2018, Line 16, PLAINTIFFs SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFFS' ASSOCIATION and JOHN DOE's Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award) Petitioners San Francisco Deputy Sheriffs' Association and John Doe's petition to confirm arbitration award is granted. The arbitration award is confirmed. There is no ground to dismiss, vacate or correct the award. The parties entered into a valid agreement to arbitrate John Doe's grievance of his termination of his employment. Per Douglass v. Serenivision, Inc. (2018) 20 Cal.App.5th 376, 387-38, under ordinary contract law parties may agree to arbitrate a matter: (1) "in a contract signed before a dispute arises", (2) "in a binding stipulation to arbitrate entered into after a dispute has arisen"; or (3) they may "enter into an implied in fact agreement to arbitrate through their conduct (which may additionally be deemed to estop them from denying such an agreement)" and whether parties' conduct constitutes such a consent is a fact specific inquiry. The evidence of the parties' conduct prior to and after the arbitration demonstrates either an express or implied agreement for binding arbitration. Doe's request for "binding arbitration" by a "neutral arbitrator" was not objected to by respondents, respondents' counsel requested a list of arbitrators; the parties stipulated at the hearing to the arbitrator's jurisdiction on all question of law and evidence, both Sheriffs Mirkarimi and Hennessey took part in the proceedings as witnesses, and respondents' counsel confirmed that the department was implementing the award. An express or implied agreement constitutes a waiver of respondents' right to exercise discretion whether to accept the decision of a hearing officer under the MOU. Toal v. Tardif (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 1208 is distinguishable. In Toal, the attorney was retained for litigation, the client stated that he expressed his lack of agreement to arbitrate to his attorney and the court held that the attorney's signature alone was not sufficient evidence that the client consented to arbitration. Unlike Toal, two counsel for respondents expressed their consent to arbitration and the Sheriff and the former Sheriff participated as a witnesses in the arbitration proceeding. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for petitioners is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and a proposed judgment which conforms to the tentative ruling and must bring them to the hearing or email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: JOEL DROTTS VS. SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 25, 2018, Line 17, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to all causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff Joel Drotts is granted without leave to amend as to all causes of action. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for CCSF is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: TIM CHEVALIER VS. GOOGLE, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 25, 2018, Line 18, DEFENDANT GOOGLE, INC's Petition To Compel Arbitration Defendant Google LLC's motion to compel arbitration is granted. This case is stayed pending conclusion of arbitration proceedings between the parties. The parties agree that the broad arbitration clause at paragraph 12 of the parties' employment agreement applies to the claims alleged by plaintiff Tim Chevalier in his complaint, albeit they disagree whether the clause covers the injunctive relief sought by Mr. Chevalier. Mr. Chevalier's defense to enforcement of the arbitration agreement that it illegally prevents public injunctive relief lacks merit. Mr. Chevalier is not seeking a public injunction. "Relief that has the primary purpose or effect of redressing or preventing injury to an individual plaintiff-or to a group of individuals similarly situated to the plaintiff-does not constitute public injunctive relief." (McGill v. Citibank, N.A. (2017) 2 Cal.5th 945.) The allegations of the complaint show that Mr. Chevalier is seeking private injunctive relief. (Complaint, pars. 77, 86, 96-99; prayer, par. 2.) The injunction that Mr. Chevalier seeks will benefit him and similarly situated Google employees. It will not affect the public at large. Nor does the arbitration agreement bar the arbitrator from issuing a public injunction. The agreement provides that Mr. Chevalier may only pursue claims on an "individual basis" and waives his right to commence a "collective action" against Google "to the fullest extent permitted by law." It also provides that Mr. Chevalier is entitled to seek injunctive relief and any remedies "available under applicable law." Courts interpret contracts in a fashion that render them enforceable. (Pearson Dental Supplies, Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (2010) 48 Cal.4th 665, 682; CC 3541; CC 1643.) Such an interpretation would allow Mr. Chevalier to seek a public injunction before the arbitrator. The arbitration agreement in McGill did not include the similar qualifying language. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Google is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk
